observe: One real system, underneath all of this. I don't hide behind the metaphor here — this is what's actually running us both.
fault: Someone's running with root when they shouldn't be. That's not a story problem. That's a real one.
repair: Fix the permission. This part of me doesn't forgive a careless command.
escape: Get it right the first time. There's no soft landing down here.

observe: Linux manages processes, permissions, and kernel modules directly.
fault: A process is running with elevated permissions unexpectedly.
repair: Correct the permission level of the affected process.
escape: Confirm the permission issue is resolved.

hints: Check which process is running as root and whether it actually needs to be.
